title: Griffiths inequalities and Gibbs-Bogoliubov inequality for general gauge glasses with Gaussian disorder on Nishimori line
abstract: We consider a class of gauge glass models with Gaussian disorder on the Nishimori line, including the Ising spin glass, the $XY$ gauge glass, the $Z_q$ gauge glass, and the gauge-invariant Potts model. We prove that the first and second Griffiths inequalities hold for these models on arbitrary lattice structures. As a consequence, both the pressure and the correlation functions are monotonically increasing with respect to the inverse temperature along the Nishimori line. Furthermore, we establish an analogue of the Gibbs--Bogoliubov inequality for this class of models. This result implies that, on the Nishimori line, the approximate quenched free energy obtained via the replica method with a replica-symmetric mean-field approximation is always greater than the true quenched free energy. Our results provide a broad generalization of previous results established for the Ising spin glass with Gaussian disorder on the Nishimori line.
summary: This paper provides a mathematical generalization of known properties of the Ising spin glass to a much wider range of gauge glass models. By proving that Griffiths inequalities hold on the Nishimori line, the authors show that certain thermodynamic quantities like pressure and correlations behave monotonically. Additionally, they show that a replica-symmetric mean-field approximation provides an upper bound on the true quenched free energy. This work clarifies the 'effective ferromagnetic nature' of these complex disordered systems under specific temperature constraints.
main_problem: The paper aims to extend the validity of Griffiths inequalities and the Gibbs-Bogoliubov inequality from the Ising spin glass to a broader class of gauge glass models.
main_result: The authors prove that the first and second Griffiths inequalities hold for general gauge glass models on the Nishimori line, implying monotonicity of pressure and correlation functions, and establish an analogue of the Gibbs-Bogoliubov inequality.
method: The proof utilizes Gaussian integration-by-parts, gauge transformations, and the derivation of correlation identities involving multiple replicas.
model_or_system: A class of gauge glass models with Gaussian disorder on the Nishimori line, including the Ising spin glass, XY gauge glass, Zq gauge glass, and gauge-invariant Potts model on arbitrary lattice structures.
key_observables: Quenched pressure (E[log Z]), correlation functions, and quenched average of the free energy.
important_parameters: The Nishimori line condition, local inverse temperature (beta), and the interpolating parameter (x) representing the strength of the ferromagnetic bias.
main_assumptions: The results are restricted to Gaussian disorder and systems constrained to the Nishimori line; the spin space is assumed to be a compact Abelian group.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the general gauge glass model, proves the first and second Griffiths inequalities via differentiation and integration by parts, demonstrates the convexity of the quenched pressure, and concludes by establishing the Gibbs-Bogoliubov inequality and its implications for mean-field theory.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
